entry.title: How to use SAP Joule in Non SAP Applications
entry.content: We are exploring the use of Joule within our non-SAP application. Could anyone please explain the integration process? Specifically, is there an API available that allows us to consume Joule services directly in our application, without relying on SAP Datasphere?

entry.title: node rfc server issue being unreliable
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I set up a node rfc server according to the official documentation. https://github.com/SAP-archive/node-rfc/blob/HEAD/doc/usage.md#nodejs-server-program-example

I set up the ini file, the rfc destination (which successfully passes the connection test), the sec_info, and reg_info.

I'm calling the Node.js RFC function from ABAP using a CALL FUNCTION ... DESTINATION statement.

The issue is sometimes it works and sometimes not. Once it executes the node js function I defined successfully then I restart the server again and then I get the error that the function module (name of the function) not found.

I could not find a reasonable pattern that under what condition it works and under what condition it does not.

I appreciate any help.

I can provide my full codes if needed.

Thanks in advance

@Srdjan

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Hello Everyone

entry.title: Universal Allocations- Insurance Company
entry.content:
Hello Community, 

We are an Insurance Company looking to implement Fiori Universal Allocations apps to replace our current custom allocations process. Is there anyone currently working with/for an insurance company that uses these apps and would like to connect? We are looking to discuss how another insurance company uses these apps given the state/national guidelines in insurance reporting. 

Thanks!

entry.title: Certification and Beyond: Stories from SAP’s Top Trainers
entry.content:
Tinus Brink and Lisa Merrill have a lot in common: they both work as SAP consultants and trainers; were both commended as SAP’s highest achieving trainers in the 2024 SAP Authorized Trainer Program; and they’re both passionate about their respective hobbies. But the similarities don’t end there. Their deep-rooted commitment for lifelong learning and inspiring others through education shines through everything they do. And both embrace continuous learning through SAP Learning Hub, balancing their professional expertise with rich personal lives and unique interests.  

From Family Support to Global Impact 

Tinus’ SAP career began thanks to his mother-in-law, who took a leap of faith in him and backed his initial journey with a loan on her home. And her belief in him and in SAP paid off. Today, over 20 of Tinus’ family members and friends now work with SAP in some capacity.  

Originally from South Africa, Tinus has been living in Texas for two years and calls it home. With five SAP Certifications under his belt, ‘Tiny’, as he is affectionately known by those he trains, believes knowledge truly is power: “Being the consultant with the answers not only builds confidence but also strengthens your ability to support clients effectively,” he says. “The skills we gain through SAP training unlock new opportunities for growth and success”.  

Over the years, Tinus has had the privilege of participating in inspiring programs like SAP Skills for Africa, the SAP Young Professional Program, and the Dual Study initiative with various universities.  But his love of learning and training extends outside of the workplace. When he’s not exploring new technologies and experimenting with software and AI, he’s coaching rugby in local schools.  

“Supporting others in their growth and development has always been at the heart of my work”, he explains. “I take great pride in helping new talent enter the SAP ecosystem and kick-start their careers”. And it’s never been easier. The flexibility of remote learning through SAP Learning Hub fits perfectly with his lifestyle: “I can study on weekends or evenings without disrupting family time. I’ve already planned two new certifications this year.” 

Helping Others Dive Deeper Into Their Potential 

Lisa Merrill, an SAP consultant and SAP Authorized Trainer from Chicago agrees. “SAP Learning Hub has been a wonderful tool and there are days when I wonder how we lived without it. I am a better trainer because I have a place that I can go to 24/7 to read, review, and practice.”   

Like Tinus, Lisa’s love of learning opened new doors beyond her career. In 1997, while working on a global SAP implementation, she was introduced to the world of scuba diving, completed a course, and it has been a passion ever since. Today, when Lisa isn’t diving 30-120 feet below sea level in the Bahamas with her family, she’s diving into a new project or training a new intake of SAP users in a classroom.  

As her SAP skills deepened, so did her impact. Over her 25+ year career, Lisa has been fortunate to work on consulting projects as well as provide training to SAP clients around the globe. But a clear highlight is her involvement in SAP’s NS2 Serves initiative that provides military veterans with SAP training, certification, and career opportunities after service. The program provides them with new skills and a fresh start. “It is an honor and a privilege,” she fondly recalls. “Having the opportunity to help veterans prepare for SAP Certifications and later hear about the impact it has made on their lives is incredibly rewarding and fulfilling”. 

Lisa is a firm believer that a lack of training is one of the main reasons projects aren’t successful.  Over the years she has seen companies design great systems that never reach their potential because users aren’t sufficiently trained in the systems. “When I train, I hope, in some small way, that I am contributing to the overall success of an implementation,” she shares. “My motto is: When you stop learning, you stop evolving”.   

A Path to Purpose 

For both Lisa and Tinus, SAP Certification wasn’t just a checkbox. It validated their knowledge, sharpened their skills, and opened meaningful new opportunities to support others on their SAP journeys. With SAP Learning Hub, they’ve been able to stay current, fuel their innate curiosity, and continue doing what they both love most: helping others succeed. 

You too can benefit from self-paced learning opportunities that meet you how you learn best. With SAP Learning Hub, you can unlock guided learning resources including live sessions, practice systems and everything you need to get and stay certified and boost your career.

entry.title: Re: Instance creation failed - abap trial
entry.content:
@Andre_Fischer, Hello. I followed as explained, still I am facing the same issue, date and time also in the attached screenshot.

I tried multiple times closing and reopening browser. Should I proceed again with deleting trial and recreating?

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK Create Media action failing on IOS platform f...
entry.content:
Are you working with an online or offline OData service?

Looking at the CreateMedia action you appear to be passing the attachment content in the Headers as well as defining as the Media.  You should only be specifying it as the Media.   Headers are strings and the error may be due to trying to pass binary data in the header.  Try removing the Headers/Content and see if that fixes it.

entry.title: Offcycle event batch to "termination details".
entry.content: In SuccessFactors we have offcycle event batch for job information and employment details section. Can't that be added to termination details as well? Because to the standard field "Ok To Rehire" I think it should not always be a manual data change. For example in our client side we have a rule saying that a user cannot be "ok to rehire = NO" for more than 20 years. So they suggested us to make make an automation based on a custom date field where they decide when the employee should be ok to be rehired again. It's obviously going to be a future dated field and there is no rules to make it happen. So off cycle event batch could have been a good option if that was feasible. Currently we might have to do it manually.

entry.title: Re: unable to see in HTML5 Application in SAP BTP COCKPIT...
entry.content: done many times still the same

entry.title: Re: Errors in Onboarding 2.0 trying to add or update documents
entry.content: Hi, SAP Support initially found the issue was from a backend configuration toggle that was inadvertently disabled (no idea how this happened). The SAP Engineering team promptly addressed this by enabling the correct setting. Then they needed to validate and reconfigure the PFS user ID and password exactly without any hidden characters or spaces. Then all worked fine again.

entry.title: Re: Next Inspection Date not updated during 301 movement...
entry.content:
Hello there

According to SAP note 3291907,  'Next Inspection Date' is updated:

  1. At the time of Goods Receipt when the field 'Next inspection' is initial for all stock relevant inspection lots (inspection origin 01, 04, 05 and 08).
  2. At the time of Usage decision for recurring inspection lots (origin 09).

In fact, stock transfer postings are specifically excluded in the code:  CHECK dm07m-kzuml IS INITIAL. Which means even an implementation of BAdI MB_BATCH_MASTER method MAINTAIN_INSPECTION_DATE  will not help.

To be perfectly honest,  I don't fully understand why you would want stock transfers to update the 'Next Inspection Date'?

entry.title: Re: Issue with WRITE statement
entry.content:
Date format would initially come from user master record, and a SET COUNTRY will override it. So look for

  • Same user or not, especially if a technical user generates the output in background?
  • There is a SET COUNTRY in form GET_DATA check actual value(s)
*--- Set Country for display conversions e.g. WRITE TO
  set country <gs_vbdkr>-land1.​

entry.title: Re: VBAP-AUFNR missing in VA01 depending on item category
entry.content:
Normally, if the sale order is integrated with an account assignment objects like

  • an internal order or
  • wbs element or
  • if the sale order is relevant for CO or
  • make-to-stock production with Product Cost by Sales Order

entry.title: Re: Intercompany billing - IDoc segment E1EDP02 021 missi...
entry.content:
Hi Guys, 

   I guess the BADI GV_BADI_SD_INVOICE_IDOC_OUTPUT can be used to add a new entry into the segment E1EDP02 with 021 qualifier (or any other that qualifier for that matter). I will be talking to our development team to activate this BADI so I can test if my assumption is correct.

Good luck,

Marcelo Chamon

entry.title: Re: Filed" scrap "in routing masterdata does not exist in...
entry.content:
Hello,

The Scrap (%) field in routing operations is not included in the standard migration template in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, and cannot be mass-updated after migration.
Your options:

Manual Update
Use the Change Routing app to update Scrap % manually (works for small volumes).

API Update
Use API_PRODUCTION_ROUTING_2 (if available in your tenant) to update scrap via script or integration.

Custom App
Build a small BTP or Fiori app to manage scrap values via API (best for ongoing updates at scale).

Template modification or mass import isn't supported for this field in Public Cloud.

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK Create Media action failing on IOS platform f...
entry.content: Hi Bill, Thanks for replying. The Content header is a mistake in the screenshot as I have removed it and tried this approach before, but still the same error persists. I'm working with a online OData service. Please let me know what further action I can try to make this work.

entry.title: Re: Where can I find business process BPMN diagrams relev...
entry.content:
You will find many of them at Scope Item level, whether for;

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: J58 Accounting and Financial Close https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2502/SolP/J58?region=DE

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: J58 Accounting and Financial Close https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-055/2023-FPS02/SolP/J58?region=DE

SF EC Employee Central Replication to ERP HCM Payroll: (4AL) https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-016/2405/SolP/4AL?region=DE

As already pointed out, not all industry solutions might have them..

entry.title: Re: How to access and modify the data in QM01 from user-e...
entry.content:
In this enhancement, the FM EXIT_SAPMIWO0_008 should copy the values of I_VIQMEL to the shared area VIQMEL, meaning the Dynpro is already able to display these values.
Be aware that the PAI exit EXIT_SAPMIWO0_009 copies I_VIQMEL to E_VIQMEL before your code copies data from your subscreen to E_VIQMEL.

Have you tried accessing purchase order item data from memory (or the database in case of failure) using an FM such as ME_EKPO_SINGLE_READ (without refreshing or bypassing the buffer), or using a dirty assignment to memory? Have you also checked memory IDs such as MAT and WRK?

entry.title: Re: Overhead Accounting with Universal Parallel Accounting in SAP S/4HANA 2022
entry.content:
Dear Janet,

Thank you in advance for every information you share with everyone and this let us know about this new funcionality deeply.

So, please, let me ask you a question about event-based posting - overhead calculation for internal order with Universal Parallel Accounting. 

According to the Help SAP information: Once you've defined an event-based costing sheet and assigned it to your internal order as described in prerequisites, an overhead calculation is directly carried out for each business transaction. If the posting of a business transaction includes a posting that matches a cost element of the cost basis, an overhead amount is calculated based on the corresponding percentage rate. The result is posted in a separate journal entry with the Actual overhead (realtime) - KZPR business transaction type.

We're testing this and it's working but with some exceptions, for example, we're seing that this option (event-based) only posted in the leading ledger - this means that for a journal entry with just a parallel ledger (journal entry example, posted with "Post general journal entries" app in a parallel ledger and not leading-ledger) the system doesn't calculate any overhead and it doesn't create the separate journal entry with the Actual overhead (realtime) - KZPR business transaction type.

Is it work like this?, is that correct?

I would highly appreciate your advice.

Thank you and best regards,

Estefanía.

entry.title: SAP Process Automation: Simplifying Business Workflow
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced digital environment, businesses are constantly looking for ways to work smarter and faster. That’s where SAP Process Automation comes in. Whether you're working in finance, HR, procurement, or supply chain, SAP Process Automation can help you get rid of repetitive tasks and streamline your operations. But what exactly is it, and why should you care?

Let’s break it down.

 What is SAP Process Automation?

SAP Process Automation is a solution that allows organizations to automate business processes without writing complex code. It combines the power of workflow management and robotic process automation (RPA) into a single, easy-to-use platform.

Put simply, it helps you:

  • Design business workflows
  • Automate repetitive tasks (like data entry)
  • Reduce manual errors

Free up employees to focus on higher-value work

 Key Features

Here are some of the most useful capabilities of SAP Process Automation:

1. Drag-and-Drop Workflow Builder

Non-technical users can design business processes visually using a simple interface—no coding needed.

2. Bots (RPA)

Software bots can mimic human actions like clicking buttons, filling forms, or copying data from one app to another.

3. Pre-Built Content

SAP offers a library of pre-built workflows and bots for common business scenarios like invoice processing, employee onboarding, or purchase order creation.

4. Integration with SAP and Non-SAP Systems

The tool works seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA and can also interact with external systems like Excel, email, or web portals.

 Real-World Use Cases

Here are a few examples where SAP Process Automation shines:

  •  Finance: Automating vendor invoice approvals and payments
  • HR: Handling employee leave approvals or document generation
  •  Procurement: Automating purchase requisitions and purchase order processing
  • IT: Auto-resolving simple support tickets or resetting passwords

 Benefits of SAP Process Automation

Benefit Description

  • Saves Time Replaces manual, repetitive tasks with automated flows
  •  Reduces Errors Ensures data accuracy and compliance
  •  Cost-Efficient Saves costs by reducing the need for human intervention
  • Scalable Easily adapts as business needs grow
  • Secure Enterprise-grade security and access controls

Getting Started

To begin using SAP Process Automation, you typically need:

  • An SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) account
  • Access to the SAP Process Automation service
  • Basic understanding of your business process workflows
  • Training or help from SAP documentation or partners

 SAP Process Automation vs Traditional Automation

  • Aspect SAP Process Automation Traditional RPA
  • Integration with SAP Deep integration May need connectors
  • Ease of Use Low-code/no-code Usually requires scripting
  • Intelligence Built-in AI/ML features Limited without add-ons
  • Scalability Cloud-native and scalable Depends on setup

Conclusion

SAP Process Automation is a game-changer for companies running on SAP. It brings together the power of workflow design and robotic automation to simplify complex operations and boost productivity.

If you're tired of chasing approvals, updating spreadsheets, or entering the same data in five different places, it might be time to explore automation.

entry.title: Re: Intercompany billing - IDoc segment E1EDP02 021 missi...
entry.content: Hi Guys, sorry it is me again. I've just checked this BADI and it is restricted to SAP use only. Then, I hope the USEREXIT EXIT_SAPLVEDF_002 could be more appropriate.

entry.title: Re: Novo membro a esse mundo louco chamado SAP

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for June – SAP CAP ❄️ 🏔️
entry.content: Glad to be safe and out - staying under a roof in summer heat should count as a survival sport 😄

entry.title: Re: Flexible workflow Supplier invoices for migrated POs
entry.content:
Hello @sanne_larsen 

This issue occurs because the migrated POs lack linkage to PRS, so the flexible workflow defaults to the PO creator instead of the requester. Manually filling in the requester doesn’t work because the workflow logic relies on PR metadata, not display fields.
Best Solution:

Use substitution rules in the “Manage Substitutions for Workflows” app. Assign the correct approver as a substitute for each PO creator so migrated invoices are routed correctly. This is quick to implement and ideal for one-time cleanup.
Optional for Future:

Using key user extensibility and workflow determination logic, you can enhance the workflow condition logic to check a custom “responsible approver” field in the PO when no PR exists.

SAP Help Reference: Manage Substitutions App

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Workflow details html download incorrect

In the html download - the l_agent should be under workflow and the direction should be opposite too.

is this error in SAP or i am interpreting it incorrectly ?

We've tried the common fixes like logging on/off, restarting cloud foundry and even creating a new account but the result is the same.

entry.title: Re: ISU_DB_QMEL_UPDATE Update QMEL table
entry.content: BAPI_ALM_NOTIF_DATA_MODIFY, BAPI_QUALNOT_MODIFY_DATA, etc.

entry.title: Is there any impact upgrading SAP GUI to 8.00 release 32-bit SAP Logon on 64-bit Windows computer?
entry.content:
Hi Dear SAP,

I have a question: What would happen if I installed 32-bit SAP Logon on my 64-bit computer?

Currently, I have 32-bit SAP Logon 770 installed. To upgrade to SAP Logon 800, I can see SAP have 32-bit and 64-bit versions.

If we upgrade to SAP Logon 800, will there be any impact?

 

Thanks.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is there any impact upgrading SAP GUI to 8.00 release...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for reaching out to SAP Support.

The answer for this question can be found in the SAP Note 66971 - Supported SAP GUI platforms:

Supported platforms for SAP GUI for Windows

  • SAP GUI for Windows 8.00:
    • Windows 10 32-bit (only Education, Pro, and Enterprise editions)
    • Windows 10 64-bit on x64 (AMD) and Intel EM64T (only Education, Pro, and Enterprise editions)
    • Windows Server 2016 64-bit on x64 (AMD) and Intel EM64T
    • Windows Server 2019 64-bit on x64 (AMD) and Intel EM64T
    • Windows 11 on x64 (AMD) and Intel EM64T (only Education, Pro, and Enterprise editions)
    • Windows Server 2022 on x64 (AMD) and Intel EM64T:
    • Windows Server 2025 on x64 (AMD) and Intel EM64T - as of patch level 11

You cannot use the 64-bit version of SAP GUI for Windows 8.00 (see SAP Notes 3075781 and 3218166) on 32-bit platforms. In addition, you cannot use it to work with old SAP products that use SAP_BASIS versions below 7.00.

Therefore, based on the above information outlined in the SAP Note, there is no restrictions to run either SAP GUI 8.00 32 bit or SAP GUI 64 bit on Windows 64-bit. The only restriction would be running SAP GUI 64bit version on a 32bit platform. 

 

Best Regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: bapi_po_create1 : passing standard table field J_1BNB...
entry.content:
In table parameters POITEM and POITEMX, the relevant field name is BRAS_NBM.

NB: Look at data element from SE11 on EKPO, 'Further Characteristics' tab, field 'Default Component Name'

entry.title: Bom dia pessoal! Tempo muito longo para receber os XML ; INBOUD.
entry.content:
Bom dia, pessoal!

Estamos enfrentando um problema com a solução INBOUND em nossa instalação.

Anteriormente, no GRC, realizávamos a importação via XML inserido diretamente pelo fornecedor. Já na nova solução DRC, da SAP, a proposta é buscar os XMLs diretamente na SEFAZ, por meio do certificado digital. A funcionalidade está operando, mas temos notado uma grande variação no tempo de resposta — os arquivos estão sendo recuperados em um intervalo que vai de 20 minutos até 4 horas, o que compromete a previsibilidade do processo.

Alguém mais está enfrentando essa situação após a migração? Existe alguma recomendação ou ajuste que possa ser feito para estabilizar ou otimizar esse tempo de busca?

Agradeço desde já pela troca!

entry.title: Re: Scheduling background job through ABAP program which...
entry.content:
You can find some samples in online help, look at the samples provided.

Either

  • Schedule a single job with 3 steps
  • Schedule 3 jobs, with start condition 2 after 1, 3 after 2, only release via BP_JOB_RELEASE the job 1 when 2 and 3 are already scheduled.

entry.title: Re: How to use SAP GUI Scripting Inside Windows PowerShell
entry.content:
Could you please explain how to call function like getCellValue or getallnodekeys

By Get-Property or InvokeMember

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Zypern
entry.content:
🌍 SAP Meetup in Cyprus – You're Invited!

Dear SAP Professionals,

I’m excited to invite you to a relaxed and inspiring SAP networking evening in Cyprus!

Whether you’re a consultant, developer, end user, or SAP enthusiast — if you work with SAP in any shape or form, you’re very welcome to join.

This is a great chance to:

  • Connect with fellow SAP experts on the island 🌴

  • Exchange ideas and experiences 🤝

  • Discuss the latest SAP trends and technologies 🚀

  • Simply enjoy a good evening in good company 🍻

No agenda, no pressure — just authentic exchange and shared passion for SAP.

Please drop a quick comment or message if you plan to attend, so I can plan accordingly.

Looking forward to meeting many of you in person!

Warm regards,
Vitaly

entry.title: Re: ABAP Test Cockpit in the Cloud – How to use baseline
entry.content:
Hi @OlgaDolinskaja ,

I am testing ATC from BTP scenario in our sandbox. Setup is done and working fine. I have created a custom variant combining ATC & CVA checks and ran the checks using custom variant. I Baselined all the findings. For a test purpose, I created a simple report with hardcoded breakpoint with user name. It showed in my findings and after baseline the finding is gone. Then I added one more line in same report with hardcoded breakpoint with another user name. Now if I run checks again, I see 2 findings for both the error lines. Any suggestion ? Does anyone else also reported similar issue ?

entry.title: Re: Class FI_TAX_SERVICES causes error RW033 after upgrade
entry.content:
Did you already check for KBA (or SP notes) such as

  • 3533461 - FAQ Tax calculation logic 
  • 1804818 - Error in Tax calculation for BAPI/IDOC, after upgrade to new Release 

NB: SP support package, but for which version?

entry.title: Learn Quickly via the SAP Activate Minute Videos
entry.content:
Did you know SAP Activate offers a series of short, focused videos to help you quickly learn key topics without a big time commitment?
This video series is called SAP Activate Minute.

You can access it through the SAP Activate Community or directly via this link.

The playlist includes over 60 bite-sized videos designed to support your SAP implementation journey with practical tips and insights.

The videos are organized by posting date, so we recommend starting from the bottom and working your way up to access the most recent content first.

Let us know what you think!

entry.title: Are there step by step guidelines in learning HUB for a specific integration scenario using BTP IS?
entry.content: Hello Experts, I am Integration consultant and new to BTP IS learning, I want to understand the Integration patterns and protocols configuration using BTP IS. Do SAP learning hub has step by step guidelines for specific Integration scenarios while working using BTP integration suite. If so can you pls let me know how to search for that.

entry.title: I wish to use the parameters datefrom and dateto in the SQL Command is this possible?
entry.content:
Hi, I’d like to confirm whether it’s possible to use Crystal Reports parameters (e.g., ?DateFrom and ?DateTo) directly in a SQL Command.

The goal is to filter program enrollments where:

  • The start date is on or before ?DateTo
  • The end date is on or after ?DateFrom (or is NULL)

This is to ensure we only include enrollments active during the report’s date range.

However, we have edge cases where one program ends on the same day another begins. In those cases, we want to select the latest enrollment that was active when a consent document was created. Since the consent event doesn’t store program info directly, we’re trying to infer it based on the event date.

Ideally, I’d use SQL ranking (e.g., ROW_NUMBER()) to pick the most relevant enrollment, but I need access to the parameter dates inside the SQL to do that correctly. From what I’ve read, this might not be possible but if it is, I’d appreciate any guidance or workarounds. I don't know what ?datefrom and to might be in play so I can't dismiss any enrollment in the sql command.

Thanks!

entry.title: integration flow
entry.content: since 2 days the i is not working any idea

entry.title: SAPUI5 in WinGUI (dialog popup, sap.m etc)
entry.content:
Assumptions: Onprem and persons  3 sp 15.   Both web and win gui users.

Scenario: We would like to enhance standard t-code iw32 to allow users to do a sapui5 dialog "popup" that has sapui5 controls on it and allows the user to select from table on the popup and have that update fields and table rows in tabs on iw32.  It's important that it works for both Wingui  and Webgui. Webgui seems to work but Wingui doesn't recognize sapui5 stuff in the script execution.  

Is there something i'm doing  wrong or is Wingui just not going to work for that?  I noticed there is some option for applets also, but it wasn't clear if that would be useful for Wingui.  

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Reforma Tributária, 2026 Valor total da NF
entry.content: Aproveitando o ensejo que estamos falando da nota 3556404, esta nota está aparecendo como pré-requisito em nota TAXBRJ (3571837) veja que essa ultima aqui é para implementar a RVXBRA. Pergunta para quem usa TAXBRJ deverá realmente implementar a nota (3556404)?? Os BCSets da nota (3556404) são para RVABRA. ou pode ser implementada, mas sem os passos manuais.

entry.title: How Cloud App Security Works in SAP BTP - Step- by-step Guide to Authentication and Authorization
entry.content:
When I started working on a cloud app using SAP BTP, one of the most challenging areas I faced was setting up security - especially authentication and authorization using XSUAA.

So I decided to write this blog to help developers who are trying to understand how cloud app security really works in SAP BTP .

1. Authentication & Authorization: - In SAP BTP Authentication and Authorization are key for Securing access to services and data, especially when binding apps with CAP (Cloud Application Program).

Authentication is handled by XSUAA. Think like this is the Entry gate of your application. It verifies the user using identity providers (IDP) .

It works like: -  i) User logs via IDP.                                                                                                                                                        ii) XSUAA generate a JWT Token                                                                                                                            iii) The app verifies the token and identifies the user.   

XSUAA (XS Advanced User Account and Authentication Service): - It is a Security Service which is Used to manage authentication.                                                                                                               i) First it will authenticate the user like who is the user then verify it.                                                           ii) Then JWT Token will generate.

JWT (JSON Web Token): - It is a small digital identity file which tells the backend -                                                                          i) Who is the user (user-id, email)                                                                                                                            ii) what is the roles (Admin, Viewer)                                                                                                                        iii) When the token is expired.                                                                                             JWT Token will validate the user is real and verified in the backend.

Authorization is basically confirms what they are allowed to do. you can assume that Access to rooms inside the building.

Step 1: - Define Security in xs-security.json :- This file basically defines the permissions (scopes) and roles your application as you can see below

 

Step 2: - Bind XSUAA in mta.yaml                                                                                                                          Next, we tell BTP that our app depends on the XSUAA service. 

 

After deployment , BTP will create a managed of XSUAA and bind to your app.

make sure that you have the mta tools installed if not then in terminal npm install -g mbt  then write cds add mta  it will add mta.yaml file.

Step 3: Protect Service with @requires: -      Now add the required scope in your service definition.

Example in srv/service.cds:    

service CatalogService {

  @requires: 'ReadData'           // This means only users who have the ReadData scope will be                                                                able to access this endpoint.

  entity Books as projection on my.Books;  }

 

Step 4: Deploy Your App

Run the following:

cds build --production

cf deploy mta_archives/your-app.mtar   This deploys your CAP project with the security config.

Note: - always double check that your xs-security.json and mta.yaml match.

Step 5: Create and Assign Role Collections: -  It is important to assign role . follow the process to assign role                                                                                                                                                             i)Navigate to your subaccount > Security > Role Collections                                                                ii)Click Create Role Collection                                                                                                                        iii)Name it ViewerCollection (or anything you like)                                                                                          iv)Add your role DataViewer from the deployed app .                                                                                v)Go to Trust Configuration, click your Identity Provider, and assign the role collection to your user.                                                                                                                                                                                   vi)After assigning a role collection, wait 1-2 minutes or re-login for it to take effect.                                  vii)Don't forget to assign roles for each identity provider (like: - SAP ID Service).

This is how you can secure your applications.

Now we will see How Authentication Works Behind the Scenes

 User opens your app                                                                                                                                         They are redirected to SAP's login page (via XSUAA)                                                                               After login, XSUAA returns a JWT token                                                                                                         CAP service checks this token on every request                                                                                               If token has ReadData scope then  access granted                                                                                         If not then 403 Forbidden                                                                                                                                         To inspect the user token, you can log req.user in a handler.

entry.title: Re: Project Glow: New Design Coming to SAP Community on Monday, June 2
entry.content:

entry.title: Reforma Tributária TAXBRJ
entry.content:
Estamos com um cliente que vai implementa reforma e possui TAXBRJ, estamos listando as OSSNOTE. Encontramos a OSSNOTE 3571837 que faz referencia a nota 3556404, como pre-requisito (essa nota e para TAXBRA/RVABRA).

Minha pergunta é se a nota 3556404 pode ser aplicada com seus BCSET ou não faria sentido aplicar essa nota para TAXBRJ/RVXBRA?

entry.title: Re: SAP BDC Free Trial Version
entry.content: Hi @nandini1601 , the BDC basic trial is now available: https://www.sap.com/products/data-cloud/trial.html

entry.title: Re: How do you implement automatic surcharge capture?
entry.content:
Hello Nico,

The response from ChatGPT looks quite interesting. But, it seems it is not in line with functionality available in SAP Ariba.
Note Enabling Total Landed Cost for Taxes and Charges in Requisitions and Purchase Orders can help here.

However, it seems it would be manual action by Requestor to input surcharge. I think Custom fields in Catalog can help with some input for user but still has to be maintained manually by User in requisition.

Thanks,
Abhijit

entry.title: What is SAP CPI? A Beginner’s Guide with a Real-Life Example
entry.content:
Hello Commuity Members! 
This is my very first blog where I’ll share simple explanations and real-time examples to help you understand SAP CPI (Cloud Platform Integration) — especially if you're just starting your journey in SAP.

First, What is Middleware?
Before talking about CPI, let’s understand what middleware means.

Imagine you order food on Swiggy or Zomato. You’re the customer, and there’s a restaurant on the other side. The app connects you (the customer) with the restaurant. You don’t talk to the restaurant directly — the app handles the communication, order, payment, and delivery updates.

Now, think of SAP CPI as a middleware in software systems. CPI stands for Cloud Platform Integration. It’s part of SAP’s Integration Suite; its job is to connect different systems, just like that food app. These systems can be SAP systems like S/4HANA or SuccessFactors, or even non-SAP systems like Salesforce or custom applications.

Here’s a real-time scenario that helped me understand SAP CPI better. Imagine a company is using SuccessFactors for employee data and another system for Payroll processing. When a new employee joins and their details are entered into SuccessFactors, that information also needs to reach the Payroll system so that their salary can be processed correctly.

This is where SAP CPI plays a major role. It takes the new employee data from SuccessFactors, transforms it if needed, and sends it to the Payroll system. CPI makes sure that the data is sent securely and reliably. It also handles errors, retries, and logs everything for tracking.

Without CPI, someone would have to manually download the employee data and upload it into the payroll system. That takes time and can lead to mistakes. But with CPI, everything happens automatically and smoothly in the background.

When I first started learning CPI, I didn’t know what iFlows were or what an adapter meant. Over time, I understood that an iFlow is simply a visual path or design that tells CPI what to do step by step — like picking up data, changing the format, and sending it to the target system. Adapters are like connectors that allow CPI to talk to different types of systems.

I found it really interesting that, unlike other SAP modules which are limited to a specific business area like HR or Finance, CPI can connect with any area. It allows you to work with different technologies, business processes, and systems. It opened up a lot of new learning opportunities for me.

I hope this small explanation helps you get a basic idea of what SAP CPI is and how it works. If you're someone who's just getting started or exploring SAP Integration, you’re not alone. We all start from zero — and grow by learning, trying, and sharing.

Now let’s talk about something that often confuses beginners — Integration Suite. SAP CPI is actually one part of a bigger umbrella called SAP Integration Suite. You can imagine Integration Suite like a toolkit, and CPI is one of the most powerful tools inside it. Along with CPI, the Integration Suite also includes tools like API Management, Integration Advisor, Open Connectors, and Event Mesh. All these tools together help businesses build smart and connected systems.

Coming to the development side — it’s simpler than you might think. We design integrations using something called an iFlow (Integration Flow). It’s a drag-and-drop style interface where we can add steps like picking data from one system, transforming it, setting conditions, adding scripts, and sending it to another system.

Most of our development work is done inside the Web UI (Web Interface) of the SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform). Here, we can build, deploy, and monitor our integrations.

We also use Groovy scripts when we want to write custom logic, and we can create simple or complex transformations using tools like the Message Mapping, XSLT Mapping, or JavaScript (though Groovy is most common in CPI).

Once the iFlow is built, we deploy it and test it using tools like Postman or sending test data manually. The CPI Monitor helps us check if the integration worked or if something failed.

Even if this sounds like a lot right now — don’t worry. I also started with zero knowledge. Slowly, by trying small things, seeing errors, reading help blogs, and asking questions — I started building working iFlows.

I hope this blog gave you a clear picture of what CPI is, what middleware means, and how we develop real-time scenarios.

#sapcpi #CloudIntegrationPlatform #Integrationsuite

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding: Onboarding Rule Configurations in Data Review Scenarios
entry.content:
Hi @piyushkumarag ,

I created an OnInit rule using Employee Information Model.

Using the example 

"Scenario 1: Rule to differentiate workflow configuration for EC hires and Onboarding hires in HRIS entities during hiring (Manage Pending Hires)"

Expectation - 

1. Want to set the email Address visibility is Onboarding to View

 

2. and the email Address visibility is MPH should be none. Which is set in the Else part.

When I test it, the first block in Onboarding is working fine but when I test the candidate in MPH , then it doesnt go to the else section. The behaviour is same as we have in Onoboarding.

Could you please help me on this.

Thanks,

Parul

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 in WinGUI (dialog popup, sap.m etc)
entry.content:
SAPUI5 applets in Personas are a Slipstream Engine-only feature.

Also, you should upgrade that SP15 to a supported one.

entry.title: Organisational Unit - Structural Dimension configuration
entry.content:
Hi @YSE-1 

I'm trying to build Organisational Unit as a Structural Dimension, but I don't know how. This is what we have in Data Specification document. In the template, there is only Location unit as an example. Do you have a template/configuration that I can copy/adjust to my configuration for this? 

Thank you!

entry.title: Build a Simple CRUD Application with SAP Build on SAP BTP Cockpit
entry.content:
Introduction
In today’s application landscape, CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) functionalities serve as the backbone of most business solutions. SAP Build Apps, available via SAP BTP Cockpit, allows developers and business users alike to rapidly design and deploy full-stack applications using a no-code/low-code approach. This blog guides you through the steps to develop a basic CRUD app and manage it within SAP BTP Cockpit.

Prerequisites
Before proceeding, make sure you have the following in place:
An active SAP BTP account (Free Tier or Enterprise)
SAP Build Apps service enabled in your subaccount
Access to SAP BTP Cockpit
Familiarity with basic data structures (Entities and Fields)

Step 1: Prepare Environment in SAP BTP Cockpit
Log in to the SAP BTP Cockpit.
Create a new subaccount and select your preferred region.
Navigate to the Service Marketplace and enable SAP Build Apps.
Assign the necessary roles such as SAP Build Apps Admin and Developer.
(Optional) Set up destinations if you plan to connect external services or backends.

Step 2: Start Your Application in SAP Build Apps
Open SAP Build Apps from the BTP Cockpit.
Click on Create New App.
Provide a name (e.g., “Task Manager”) and choose a layout.
Create a Data Resource, either using local storage or a connected data source.
Add a Data Entity (e.g., "Tasks") with fields like id, title, description, and status.

Step 3: Create the UI for CRUD Features
Create:
Add a form with input fields for each task attribute.
Use a "Create" button with logic to insert new records into the "Tasks" entity.
Read:
Design a container with a Repeat with binding to list all tasks.
Map data fields to their respective UI components.
Update:
Design a Detail View for editing selected task data.
Pre-populate form fields and connect an "Update" button with update logic.
Delete:
Add a Delete button or icon beside each task item.
Connect it to Delete Record logic and include a confirmation dialog.

Step 4: (Optional) Integrate with External Backend
If connecting to CAPM or external APIs, define a Destination in the SAP BTP Cockpit.
Set up proper authentication and headers.
Use REST API Direct Integration in SAP Build Apps to map backend services to CRUD actions.

Step 5: Preview and Test Your Application
Use the Preview App option within SAP Build Apps.
Test all features — create, view, edit, and delete tasks.
Utilize alerts and console logging for debugging and validation.

Step 6: Deploy and Monitor Using SAP BTP Cockpit
Publish your application for web or mobile use.
Return to the BTP Cockpit to:
Monitor usage and logs
Manage authorizations and roles
Verify destination and connectivity settings

Conclusion
Congratulations! You’ve successfully created a working CRUD application using SAP Build Apps within SAP BTP Cockpit. This streamlined process enhances productivity, minimizes development time, and provides seamless SAP ecosystem integration.

entry.title: Error while loading the details of the integration flow. Try opening the artifact again. If the issu
entry.content:
I am facing this issue from past 4 days in the trial Integration suite while opening the existing artifact or while try to create a new iflow artifact in SAP CPI.

The Add & Add and Open in Editor options are not working to create a new iflow artifacts inside an integration Suite Design section. Please find the attached screenshot for your reference.

@SAPSupport  Can you please look into this issue and do needful.

Thanks,

Subba

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 in WinGUI (dialog popup, sap.m etc)
entry.content: well, i'm trying to figure out if personas or slipstream will help with the scenario described for wingui. More of a clarification if i should even be considering this approach if windgui isn't supported for sapui5 controls. Any clarifications?

entry.title: Re: Onboarding/Offboarding Tasks Assigned to all users in...
entry.content: Hi @Sang ,From the Admin Centre, navigate to Check Tool and select Application Onboarding. There, you’ll see that incorrect dynamic groups have been assigned to the Onboarding Responsible Group. You can use the Quick Fix feature within the Check Tool to remove all incorrectly assigned dynamic groups.

entry.title: Re: Career Site Builder Unified Data Model: New Job Search, Layout and Design
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I´m facing the same issue related to having two job location fields in the requisition template.

Has anyone here tried some integration to update this field automatically, because for recruiters, fill in the field related to location is not good for our business.

Thanks

entry.title: LMS Organization Connector File - All Levels
entry.content:
Hello,

Does anyone have an integration center job created for the Organization Connector, that includes multiple levels (I.E., Legal Entity, Business Unit, Department, Division) and does not cause duplication exceptions during every run?

The goal is to have ONE Org file, that gets loaded into LMS on a recurring basis, but that includes the hierarchy and not only the one lowest level of the organization. This is because having all levels provides more flexibility when using Assignment Profiles - for example by being able to select a Business Unit and simply using the 'Include Sub Entities Of' operator to also gather all departments and divisions underneath it.

As of right now, we use multiple different integration center jobs for each level, but this is not the best solution as multiple files have to be accounted for/managed.

Any insight on the Organization Connector input file is greatly appreciated! 

Thank you

Melanie

entry.title: Re: Welcome to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025
entry.content:
Thank you for sharing.

UNV is no longer supported in BI 2025. We will be converting our UNVs to UNX which is not a big deal. But we have 1000s of public reports and + we have many personal and inbox documents. Is there a tool in SAP BI that will allow us to repoint the webi reports from UNV to UNX? I know there is WorkFlow assistant but its very flaky. Will SAP be providing a tool for easy bulk repointing of the data source from uNV to UNX for the webi reports? Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Project Glow: New Design Coming to SAP Community on Monday, June 2
entry.content:
Hi @AnnePetteroe 

There is an issue with the order in which the comments are displayed for most of the old questions/posts on this platform. 

Comments are displayed  in a haphazard order and don't seem to follow a pattern like chronological. There is also no option given to us to sort the comments (chronologically or by kudos, etc.). This makes it difficult for anyone to make sense of the comments posted on let's say 1st Nov 2015 because all the comments show the same date and no time-stamp. The more the number of comments more confusing it is for us.

For instance, take this Question/Post from 2012 which has just 4 comments. That post is displayed to me as below (screenshot attached), where the comments are not chronologically sorted. Now imagine the problem if there are more than 10 comments.

This problem has been there in the old design too. Not sure if has to do with my account or region settings or if it is a actual intended functionality of this platform. I hope it gets resolved soon.

Thanks,

entry.title: Manage Price - Sales : Sales and distribution fields not filled in result grid
entry.content:
Hi,

Using Manage Price - Sales, our people want to be able to see the fields Sector, Sub-Sector, Product Family, Distribution and Material Group 5.

I can't seem to add image in my post, but in summary we do have values for those fields in "Display Material" for our distribution channel.

When we use the button settings in  "Manage Price - Sales", we can add the columns Sector, Sub-Sector, Product Family, Distribution and Material Group 5 but no data appear in those columns.

From what i saw on another post, this application is using OData API SD_PRICING_CONDITIONRECORD_SRV service. Internally for the app, SAP makes use of SD_PRICING_CONDITIONRECORD_SRV, which in turn calls on C_SLSPRICINGCONDITIONRECORDTP, a local Business Object Interface which retrieves the right data.

So i checked the view C_SLSPRICINGCONDITIONRECORDTP and its children and effectively i didn't find the missing fields anywhere in the view or its child views...

So my question is:

Are those fields really not part of the result data for "Manage Price - Sales" ?

If so, why can we choose those fields in "settings" of the grid ?

Is there a note to show those fields ?

If not, is it possible to create a ZC_SLSPRICINGCONDITIONRECORDTP ? If so, is it a big development ?

Thanks!

PS. In "Manage Price - Sales", the status of the Condition Record is "Released" and i gave myself the "SAP_ALL" permission in our QA environment just in case it was a permission problem.

entry.title: Re: Cash and Liquidity Management: Memo Records
entry.content:
Hi, is there any way to modify the planning level after the memo record is created?

my Business Requirement is that the Memo Records need to be created with different planning levels. As the Memo Records are the source for Cash management data

entry.title: SAP XPERIENCE CWB - 1º Edition - Hands-on - Workflow com o SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
O SAP Xperience CWB vai te colocar no centro do ecossistema SAP, com uma manhã totalmente mão na massa, cheia de experimentação, inovação e conexões de alto nível. 

Com uma abordagem 100% hands-on, o SAP Xperience CWB 2025 vai reunir profissionais que buscam se conectar com a inovação, aplicar conhecimentos em cenários reais de negócio e fortalecer suas competências técnicas.

Se você é do tipo que curte colocar a mão na massa, esse evento é pra você!

  • Workflow com SAP Build Process Automation
  • Conteúdo prático com foco em inovação
  • Conexão com profissionais que vivem o ecossistema SAP

Agenda:

  1. Overview SAP Community - Curador SAP Community CWB
  2. Hands-on Workflow com o SAP Build Process Automation - Mentor: @LuizSouza 

Quando/Onde:

  • 05 de julho na PUCPR – Escola Politécnica das 08h30 às 12h30
    40 vagas — garanta já a sua!

Pré-requisitos (Recomendado):

  • Conhecimento básico em SAP BTP
  • Noções de processos de negócios (ex: aprovação, requisições)

Obrigatório levar notebook para execução das atividades;

Inscreva-se e venha viver essa experiência com a gente!
https://lnkd.in/dMpp3kJS

Organização: Simone Azevedo Tiago Felipe Luiz Carlos de Souza Adriano Marques de Oliveira

entry.title: Re: How to edit a process during approval workflow withou...
entry.content: Thanks, Adriana- so I guess at this point there's nothing I can do. I would like to make a suggestion that the process builder in Solution Governance allow us to capture and set the process version that is in focus for the case during the case so that we can allow in- process edits like the situation I describe above.

entry.title: Vertical scrollbar jumps to end.
entry.content:
Hello,

In GUI, I have screen which contains data in ALV tree, and its corresponding rows have checkbox. When I am selecting or deselecting the checkbox, then scrollbar is jumping to end. This issue is happening only for few entries like when i am selecting/deselecting first 8 checkbox then scrollbar jumps to end. Whats the issue here? where should I look for this kind of issue? any hint?

entry.title: Re: Company Code fields for BP role FI Customer (FLCU00) grayed out in S/4 HANA
entry.content: Under the Master Data Synchronization- Synchronization Control-Activate Synchronization Options with update Source object BP - Target Object Customer and Active Indicator then your problem related Grayout Field into FLCU00 and FLCU01 will resolve

entry.title: Re: AI Launchpad, Generic Secret Creation Error, Creation...
entry.content:
Hi 

my suggestion:

plz create a service key in BTP cockpit to your S3 Object Store instance, then fill the values in based 64 encoded.

{"access_key_id": "base64encoded",
"bucket": "base64encoded",
"host": "base64encoded", 
"region": "base64encoded", 
"secret_access_key": "base64encoded", 
"uri":"base64encoded",
"username":"base64encoded"}

  Regards

but only when we are loggin into german, with other languages this is not an issue....

do you know anything about that?

thank you and BR

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Boa tarde Comunidade!

Sou Nilcelhe Pierin Ferraz, moro em São Paulo Brasil, trabalho com ERP da SAP há 10 anos e estou muito feliz em fazer parte da comunidade. Sou formada em Administração, especialização em Suprimentos e estou me aventurando na área de consultoria do ERP da SAP no módulo MM após uma pausa para maternidade.

entry.title: Re: Licence for SAP New Group Reporting
entry.content: What about the SAC component how is that licensed?

entry.title: Re: CJIA report payment issue
entry.content:
Hi Volodymyr!

This report simply lists all individual line items. The responsibility for evaluating and analyzing the listed data lies with the user. It is up to the user to assess which items are still unpaid, which have already been requested, and which have been settled.

The purpose of the report is solely to provide a complete list of entries, allowing the user to carry out their own analysis based on their specific needs.

Regards
Marcio Blos

entry.title: Purchase Order Message Sending for Contact Person Error "Communication type cannot be used" (SVN000)
entry.content:
Hi together

when creating a purchase order and trying to get the message sent for the contact person of the supplier, the error message "Communication type cannot be used" Err. (SVN000) comes up....

The CP has a mail adress maintained, the CP-Role and the relationship to the supplier... the customizing for the message type etc. should be also fine from our point of view

I dont know whats still missing...

thank you and BR

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Cloud or On-Premise? Not an “either or” option, but a str
entry.content:
Hi Erika,
I want to ask question, my customer need maintenance strategy of DRC cloud edition.

There seems no maintenance information available in the PAM.

https://support.sap.com/en/release-upgrade-maintenance.html

Could you kindly share where I can find it?

Kind regards,

Yasuko

entry.title: ERROR : While using OData adapter to fetch MPL to make report.
entry.content:
Hi @everyone

I hope your doing well.

I am working with OData adapter to fetch MPLs from CPI. Strangely while i am triggering the flow, It is keep on processing state and at last it is going to Abandoned status. 

My analysis on this, While i am trying to fetch less data records i.e appx 1000 it is fine. While i am trying to fetch more it is going in processing.

My requirement, I need to fetch the MPLs and need to sort out the records based on Iflow names(There are 45 Iflows, It will be given dynamically what ever is needed.) and TimeStamp. I have to make a report and show the success and failure count for dynamically given ifows. So, to achieve this first i need to fetch MPL records based on time stamp. There are more than 1000 records.

HELP. So, Please let me know how can i achieve this to fetch high data records.

I am attaching the Iflow snips.  

SAP HANA service for SAP BTP SAP Cloud Platform, industry edition, client libraries OData SAP Integration Suite

entry.title: Industry fields in the Customer/Supplier master data not populated in supplier line items
entry.content:
Hello, 

we have created new Industry fields in S4HC with SSCUI 102850, 105851, 102897

All looks good, we can see it in master data, but when checking in "Supplier line items" , where is the place we need this visible, there is only empty field. However, it is visible in Export Master data. 

Any idea?


Thank you

Lucie

entry.title: Re: Business One client does not show menu
entry.content:
Dear @frodohtx ,

From the screenshot, I infer that the users are logging into remove for B1 access.

I believe the issue might be related to memory allocation for the VM. 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: FMS query for Line total
entry.content: Could you provide more details on the requirement?

entry.title: Re: XSA Backup Restore
entry.content:
xs tenant-databases

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/fe6459d09dfa4ba1b475cbd680df2474.html

entry.title: Re: Text Field with more than characters
entry.content:
Dear @user2001 ,

First of all,  since you have created UDF as Alphanumeric-Regular, its limitation is 254 Char, you need to create UDF as Alphanumeric-Text which allows 2 GB of text in header fields and 255 KB of text in row fields.

Usually T&C are applicable for the entire document. Right click the marketing documents, you will have option for opening and closing remarks. That should be enough for the T&C.

If the T&C are are fixed text, then design them in the CR Layout to avoid users changing them in the document.

If you have requirement of T&C to be used per row item, then follow @Maya_Shiff 's suggestion.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Instance creation failed - abap trial
entry.content:
When you hover over the error button you should see some more details of the error.

If you can kindly post the same that might be helpful for us.

entry.title: Purchasing tax for Canada import
entry.content:
Hello, 

we did set all the taxes for Canada's jurisdictions, but we were not able to find the place, where to set rule for imports from USA as non taxable. 

We have two plants in Ontario and Quebec, so we have set two tax code plus one as Tax exempt. As taxes are set according to ship to address, we always see Ontario or Quebec taxation. 

Any idea, how to set Tax exempt for all orders coming from US Supplier?

We have S4HC public

Thank you

Lucie

entry.title: Removing horizontal scrollbar in SAP GUI
entry.content:
Hi, 

In SAP GUI, we have field for which search help is defined, when I am clicking on search help then I will get subscreen for selecting value, but in screen I am getting scrollbar which is unnecessary.  But once we select value and press enter its getting removed. 

What we observed, for main screen this issue is occurring in system where "Hold Scroll Position " is checked. And In system where this check is not marked, then issue is not occurring. 

Can you tell what can be cause of issue?? Does this is something to do with "Hold Scroll Position"? Can someone help with this issue?

Thank You
